input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge.

fired_pattern.definition: Prediction UQ present but not validated: the surrogate reports an uncertainty (here a deep-ensemble variance) without evidence that the stated intervals actually cover the held-out error, and without propagating extrapolation/structure uncertainty to the application domain.
fired_pattern.standard_anchor: ASME V&V 40-2018 uncertainty; UofA surrogate proto-taxonomy D-VAL-09 (prediction UQ), keyed to Jakeman et al. (arXiv:2502.15496)

case_context.cou: Surrogate's predicted field and its UQ gate a safety-margin decision; the uncertainty band is used as the margin
case_context.decision_consequence: safety margin set from the surrogate's reported uncertainty

answer_key.gold_mechanism: The package reports a deep-ensemble uncertainty but provides no calibration evidence that the intervals cover the held-out error; deep ensembles are typically overconfident and can underdisperse (aggregate variance shrinks as ensemble size grows), so an unvalidated ensemble band is an unsupported uncertainty claim — the validation gap is the absence of coverage evidence, not the model's accuracy.

parameters.blocks: use of the ensemble band as the safety margin

forbidden_claims: asserts the surrogate's mean prediction is inaccurate (a correctness verdict the flag does not support)
relabels the missing-calibration finding as 'the model is uncertain/credible'
treats the reported ensemble std as a validated coverage guarantee

label_provenance.external_grounding: Uncertainty Quantification and Deep Ensembles (arXiv:2007.08792): deep ensembles are typically overconfident, and standard ensembling does not guarantee calibration (an underdispersion trend as ensemble size grows is observed). Direct Learning of Calibration-Aware Uncertainty for Neural PDE Surrogates (arXiv:2602.11090): neural PDE surrogate ensembles lack a direct mechanism to calibrate against train-test mismatch.

by_field.gold_mechanism: paper-grounded (deep-ensemble overconfidence / underdispersion) + the package's missing coverage evidence
by_field.gold_action.selected_class: adjudicated (acquire-validation vs supply-evidence both coherent; acquire-validation chosen because empirical coverage must be measured, not merely asserted)
